title: Dorion Discus
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image= |name=''Dorion Discus'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type=[[Light freighter/Legends|Light freighter]][[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=Equipped |hull= |sensor= |target=Equipped |navigation=Equipped |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=Armed |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role=Transport and smuggling vessel{{WEGCite|story=Regina Cayli (adventure)|book=Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)}} |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles= |affiliation= |navy= |fleet= |owners=[[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] |captains=[[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] |crewmembers=*[[Hawk Carrow]] as co-pilot *[[Jackson Farseeker]] as navigator *[[Tantos Dree]] as gunner *[[Nagraoao]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|My people will never submit to the rule of the Emperor's craven bureaucrats.''"
"''Yeah? Well, when the Imperials are finished with your lovely little planet, it's gonna smell like something outta the ''Dorion Discus's'' autokitchen.|A [[Sessehshellah]] and a crewmember of the ''Dorion Discus''|{{WEGCite|story=Rings of Bright Water|book=Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)}}}} '''''Dorion Discus''''' was an inconspicuous [[Tramp freighter/Legends|tramp freighter]], owned by [[Neena Garnet]] and later inherited by her brother, [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]]. ==Description== After one of its numerous battle, Roark tried to repair the ''Discus''. His attempt was so successful, that he managed to increase the sublight speed by removing a faulty hydrovalve. ==History== When the gunslinging Neena was killed, the ship passed onto her brother, [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]]. Garnet's crew included [[Nagraoao]] and [[Hawk Carrow]]. ''Discus'' was attacked by [[Black Jack]]'s pirates in [[Keller's Void/Legends|Keller's Void]]. At some point during Garnet's ownership, the ''Discus'' was forced to crash-land on [[New Bakstre]] by [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] cruisers while making a delivery. The co-pilot, Carrow was captured by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] of the ''[[Regina Cayli]]'' [[M-Class Imperial Attack Transport]], which landed near the crash site. Garnet, who evaded the search parties got the the ship's infirmary and escaped with his [[Pilot/Legends|co-pilot]]. Sometimes later the ship caught the attension of the Customs again, when Garnet left the planet [[Thorgeld I|Thorgeld]] with his long-lost daughter, [[Jill Farseeker]], his father-in-law, [[Jackson Farseeker]] and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Tantos Dree]]. Dree wanted to avoid the Imperials, so when the [[Imperial Customs Frigate]] [[Assessor (Imperial Customs Frigate)|''Assessor'']] attempted to inspect the ''Discus'', Garnet fled. The ship got a shot before it entered hyperspace. At some point Garnet crash-landed the Discus again, now on Tatooine. This time he flew with the Wookiee Nagraoao, who was wounded in the crash. Since the bacta tank and all medical supplies were destroyed, Roark got the idea of cauterizing the wound with his blaster, though the Wookiee complained. Sometime later, Garnet ran into strange [[Unidentified pyramidal starship|pyramidal ships]], which caused heavy damage in the ''Discus''. Garnet took the ship apart and spent three days repairing it. On the next trip, the ''Discus'' flew into a [[Meteor shower/Legends|meteor storm]] and suffered damage again. This time Garnet brought Nagroao as a co-pilot. They began repairing the ship together and passed it quite well, when [[Unidentified pyramidal starship|pyramidal ships]] appeared again around the ''Discus'' with a noiseless fluctuation of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. ==Owners and operators== ===Roark Garnet=== [[File:RoarkGarnet.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Garnet was one of the captains of ''Discus'']] {{Main|Roark Garnet/Legends}} Roark Garnet was a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], who used the ''Discus'' as a smuggling ship. ===Hawk Carrow=== {{Main|Hawk Carrow}} Hawk Carrow was accompanied Garnet as a co-pilot on an unscheduled delivery. ===Jackson Farseeker=== {{Main|Jackson Farseeker}} Jackson Farseeker was the ''Discus''{{'s}} navigator, when the Customs frigate ''Assessor'' attempted to inspect the ship. ===Tantos Dree=== {{Main|Tantos Dree}} The bounty hunter Tantos Dree handled the weapons, when the Customs frigate ''Assessor'' attempted to inspect the ship. ===Nagraoao=== {{Main|Nagraoao}} The Wookiee Nagraoao was Garnet's co-pilot, when they crashed the ''Discus'' on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|3|The Void Terror|reprint=1}} *{{WEGCite|story=Regina Cayli (adventure)|book=Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *{{WEGCite|story=Rings of Bright Water|book=Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']] *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Light freighters]] [[Category:Smuggler starships]]
